item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image is used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here, which is public domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow okay.

I loved the use of the CLI for this. It felt very much like an actual Bash terminal, and makes perfect sense for it to be the interface for operating SCP-2000.

I did roll my eyes a little at —force apparently working as "ignore all errors, including segfaults" - both at such a miracle tool and its use in SCP-2000. It's like including FuckItJS in a nuclear launch system. One that can rewrite reality. (Since when can it do that, anyway?) Also, the scope defaulting to reality - if it has all these built in fallbacks for every other failure case, I would think it would check "how much stuff seems to be fucked" before defaulting to "fuck everything"

+1 for the CLI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So reality is now… bees? That pesky —force flag.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did they just… repopulate the world with bee-ple?

Holy shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I looked at another SCP that has fragments (SCP-2111) and you are correct. I thought for a moment that each offset also changed the title, but I was wrong. So yes, it is now working correctly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Offsets start at 0, so the first child becomes offset/0, the second offset/1 and so on.

I dunno, spoilers?
– hide block


If "Done" is supposed to replace the contents with the bee image, that is what it does now. Is that not correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like it's only half-fixed. It should be going to here specifically:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/fragment:scp-2000-b-the-lonely-cowboy-2/noredirect/true

But the link that you fixed it to doesn't have the title set as "Reality". It's odd…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed the offset link so it goes to "Reality".

item.title: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Tales do not get attribute tags like SCPs. They get the "tale" tag and any contest, character, or canon tags that are relevant. I have removed the attribute tags from this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I like that the doomsday wasn't really for humanity, but for humanibee. Excellent article, really enjoyed that an O5 was a scip. +1
